summary: Task 3 cleanly wires ResetReason across every ChatViewModel reset call site, gates the Persistent-bootstrap reset on first Ready via a single-shot observer mirroring observeFirstReadyThenResume, reclassifies MAX_TOKENS to Heavy (LIGHT_FIELD_LABELS purge + classifyApplyLevel `acceleratorChanged || maxTokensChanged`), and preserves UI history in applyLightOverrides. All call sites pass an explicit `reason` named argument. Out-of-scope surfaces (deriveTopAppBarState, engineReady StateFlow, ChatScreen gating) are correctly untouched. Test doubles for the six other modules and the in-file FakeModelRegistry adopt the post-Task-2 signature with `lastResetReason` + `resetReasons` recording fields. Module boundary preserved (no androidx.compose / androidx.activity in core-runtime). No blockers, no major issues; two minor robustness notes worth tracking for Task 6.

findings.issue: observeFirstReadyThenReset waits for `_uiState.first { it is ChatUiState.Ready }`, then calls `currentReadyModel()` which re-reads `registry.models.value` and demands `initStatus === ModelInitStatus.Ready`. A transient Ready→Initializing flutter that lands between the StateFlow latch and the snapshot read makes `currentReadyModel()` return null and the reset silently no-ops — KV cache stays dirty for that bootstrap. The spec's intent is that the very first Ready edge triggers exactly one reset; today the function returns without a fallback, retry, or warning log. observeFirstReadyThenResume has the same shape but its no-op is benign (auto-resume stays armed for a later Ready). For reset, this is a missed-fire window, not a wrong-fire window.
findings.suggestion: Optional hardening: log a `errorLog.w("inference-reset", "skipped reason=$reason currentReadyModel=null")` in the null branch so a flutter race is debuggable from exported logs, OR re-await the next Ready emission. Either is fine; the silent return today is the same shape as observeFirstReadyThenResume so consistency argues for leaving it. Flag for Task 6 review when engineReady StateFlow lands — the readiness primitive there could be reused here for tighter gating.

findings.issue: Two coroutines are launched in sequence on viewModelScope: `observeFirstReadyThenReset(resetReason)` and (when applicable) `observeFirstReadyThenResume(id)`. Both await the same `_uiState.first { Ready }` edge. Coroutine launch order is preserved in the queue, so reset runs first and dispatches `registry.resetConversation` before resume calls `resumePendingAssistantPersistent` — which is the desired order (fresh KV before re-running the unpaired USER turn). However this ordering is implicit (relies on launch FIFO) and would silently reverse if anyone refactors the launch into an async / parallelDecompose pattern. The KDoc on observeFirstReadyThenReset does not call out the dependency.
findings.suggestion: Add a one-line note to observeFirstReadyThenReset's KDoc (or the bootstrapChatModelId comment block at line 770-777): "Launched before observeFirstReadyThenResume so the reset settles before the unpaired-USER replay; both await the same first-Ready edge." No code change required.

findings.issue: applyLightOverrides correctly preserves UI history (`_messages`, `_streamingMessage`, `_attachments` untouched), passes `effectiveSystemPrompt(merged)` derived from the freshly merged map (not from `model.configValues` which would be racey if mutated again), and tags the reset LIGHT_OVERRIDE. The block comment at lines 455-461 is exactly the why-only style CLAUDE.md asks for. effectiveSystemPrompt has both Map and Model overloads; using the Map overload here avoids relying on the just-mutated `model.configValues` reference and is the safer choice.

classifyApplyLevel renames the local from `heavyChanged` to `acceleratorChanged` and adds `maxTokensChanged` with a Phase 3.6 Decision 4 comment. The HEAVY guard `if (acceleratorChanged || maxTokensChanged)` runs before the SEMI_LIGHT and LIGHT checks, so a HEAVY field always wins — correct precedence. The new local does not change semantics for callers that pass single-field deltas (covered by `classifyApplyLevel_returnsHeavy_forMaxTokens` and `classifyApplyLevel_returnsHeavy_forAccelerator`). LIGHT_FIELD_LABELS now contains exactly TEMPERATURE/TOPK/TOPP, matching tech-spec § Decision 4.

User-tap reset (resetConversation()) and applySystemPromptAndReset() pass `reason = ResetReason.USER` and `reason = ResetReason.SYSTEM_PROMPT` as named arguments. Every registry.resetConversation call site in this file (5 total: line 334, 463, 480, 897, plus the implicit cleanup+initialize in applyHeavySetting which doesn't call resetConversation) passes an explicit reason — no reliance on default parameters, which matches Task 2's no-default contract.

FakeModelRegistry override updates the resetConversation signature to (modelName, systemPrompt, reason), records both `lastResetReason` (singular, for single-call assertions) and `resetReasons` (list, for ordering / count assertions), and preserves the existing `lastResetSystemPrompt` + `sharedCalls += "resetConversation"` instrumentation. The two-phase test bootstrapPersistent_chatSwitchReset_waitsForReady correctly seeds Initializing, asserts empty resetReasons after advance, then publishes Ready and asserts exactly [CHAT_SWITCH] — pinning the cold-start race regression guard from tech-spec § Risks.

Six test doubles (SanctumApplicationTest, SettingsMigrationHelperTest, WarmupCoordinatorTest, DrawerViewModelTest, HomeViewModelTest, ModelManagerViewModelTest) added the `reason: ResetReason` parameter to their resetConversation override and an import of ResetReason. Mechanical change; signature compiles under the post-Task-2 interface. No behavioural assertions added — these doubles return Unit as before, which is correct since none of those tests exercise reset semantics.

Confirmed out-of-scope guards: deriveTopAppBarState body is unchanged from prior commit (still returns TopAppBarState.Draft for Draft, including Ready substate via TopAppBarState.Ready for Quick/Persistent). No engineReady StateFlow declared. ChatScreen.kt is not in this commit's diff. Module boundary check: grep `androidx.compose|androidx.activity` in core-runtime/src/main returns 0 hits — Task 3 did not touch core-runtime, and the existing UI-free invariant is preserved.
